heading: Interim application of slot rules at Chicago O’Hare International Airport
label: 41717 Interim application of slot rules at Chicago O’Hare International Airport
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this subsection, referred to in subsec. (g), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 106–181Apr. 5, 2000

Effective DateSection applicable only to fiscal years beginning after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2000 Amendments note under .Sept. 30, 1999section 3 of Pub. L. 106–181section 106 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection applicable only to fiscal years beginning after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2000 Amendments note under .Sept. 30, 1999section 3 of Pub. L. 106–181section 106 of this title

children.heading: .—Slot Operating Window Narrowed
children.label: a .—Slot Operating Window Narrowed
children.text: Effective , the requirements of subparts K and S of part 93 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations, do not apply with respect to aircraft operating before 2:45 post meridiem and after 8:14 post meridiem at Chicago O’Hare International Airport.July 1, 2001

children.heading: .—Exemptions for Air Service to Small and Nonhub Airports
children.label: b .—Exemptions for Air Service to Small and Nonhub Airports

children.text: if the air carrier was not providing such air transportation during the week of ;November 1, 1999

if the number of flights to be provided between such airports by the air carrier during any week will exceed the number of flights provided by the air carrier between such airports during the week of ; orNovember 1, 1999

if the air transportation to be provided under the exemption will be provided with a regional jet as replacement of turboprop air transportation that was being provided during the week of .November 1, 1999

children.heading: Exemptions for New Entrant and Limited Incumbent Air Carriers.—
children.label: c Exemptions for New Entrant and Limited Incumbent Air Carriers.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall grant, by order, 30 exemptions from the requirements under subparts K and S of part 93 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations, to any new entrant air carrier or limited incumbent air carrier to provide air transportation to or from Chicago O’Hare International Airport.

children.heading: .—Deadline for granting exemptions
children.label: 2 .—Deadline for granting exemptions
children.text: The Secretary shall grant an exemption under paragraph (1) within 45 days of the date of the request for such exemption if the person making the request qualifies as a new entrant air carrier or limited incumbent air carrier.

children.heading: Slots Used To Provide Turboprop Service.—
children.label: d Slots Used To Provide Turboprop Service.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), a slot used to provide turboprop air transportation that is replaced with regional jet air transportation under subsection (b)(3) may not be used, sold, leased, or otherwise transferred after the date the slot exemption is granted to replace the turboprop air transportation.

children.heading: .—Two-for-one exception
children.label: 2 .—Two-for-one exception
children.text: An air carrier that otherwise could not use 2 slots as a result of paragraph (1) may use 1 of such slots to provide air transportation.

children.heading: .—Withdrawal of slot
children.label: 3 .—Withdrawal of slot
children.text: If the Secretary determines that an air carrier that is using a slot under paragraph (2) is no longer providing the air transportation that replaced the turboprop air transportation, the Secretary shall withdraw the slot that is being used under paragraph (2).

children.heading: .—Continuation
children.label: 4 .—Continuation
children.text: If the Secretary determines that an air carrier that is using a slot under paragraph (2) is no longer providing the air transportation that replaced the turboprop air transportation with a regional jet, the Secretary shall withdraw the slot being used by the air carrier under paragraph (2) but shall allow the air carrier to continue to hold the exemption granted to the air carrier under subsection (b)(3).

children.heading: International Service at O’Hare Airport.—
children.label: e International Service at O’Hare Airport.—

children.heading: .—Termination of requirements
children.label: 1 .—Termination of requirements
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the requirements of subparts K and S of part 93 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations, shall be of no force and effect at Chicago O’Hare International Airport after , with respect to any aircraft providing foreign air transportation.May 1, 2000

children.heading: .—Exception relating to reciprocity
children.label: 2 .—Exception relating to reciprocity
children.text: The Secretary may limit access to Chicago O’Hare International Airport with respect to foreign air transportation being provided by a foreign air carrier domiciled in a country to which an air carrier provides nonstop air transportation from the United States if the country in which that carrier is domiciled does not provide reciprocal airport access for air carriers.

children.heading: 3.—StageAircraft Required
children.label: f 3.—StageAircraft Required
children.text: An exemption may not be granted under this section with respect to any aircraft that is not a Stage 3 aircraft (as defined by the Secretary).

children.heading: .—Preservation of Certain Existing Slot-Related Air Service
children.label: g .—Preservation of Certain Existing Slot-Related Air Service

children.text: before , the Secretary received a written air service termination notice for that route; orOctober 1, 1999

after , the air carrier submits an air service termination notice under section 41719 for that route and the Secretary determines that the carrier suffered excessive losses, including substantial losses on operations on that route during the calendar quarters immediately preceding submission of the notice.September 30, 1999
